description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two tall, cylindrical red pillars anchored in square gray stone bases, supporting two horizontal crossbars. The upper crossbar is slightly curved and painted black, while the lower crossbar is straight and red, connecting the two pillars. The structure is minimalist, with clean lines and no additional ornamentation, emphasizing its symbolic function as a spiritual threshold. The vivid red color is characteristic of torii gates, signifying protection and sacredness. The overall form is robust and iconic, making it a recognizable landmark in urban parks or historical districts.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red cylindrical pillars, gray stone bases, and a curved black top crossbar. The design is minimalist and symbolic, marking a sacred threshold.
description_view_0: Front view showing both red pillars, gray stone bases, and the full width of the curved black top crossbar.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the gate, with the crossbars and pillars aligned, and only one stone base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying both pillars, stone bases, and the curved black crossbar.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the slim profile and one stone base.
